title: Working
content:
Recommended quarter                                                                                         
                                         
  - P1 (S, 1-3 days): Delete or move-out-of-build the dead code — unless you're committing to Wayland-native  
  this quarter, in which case wire it through instead.                                                    
  - P2 (S, 2-4 days): Fix LinuxTicker threading, replace Sleep(1) loop with poll(), fix _isRedrawing race.
  - P3 (M, 1-2 weeks): Pass IRenderContext into SkiaView.Draw, kill SkiaRenderingEngine.Current. Do before    
  control count grows.                                                                                        
                                                                                                          
  Avoid: adding more controls or services, refactoring the handler property-mapper layer (it's correct and    
  conventional).                                                                                              
                                                 
  Open questions for the team                                                                                 
                                         
  1. Is the Skia view tree the long-term plan or a stopgap?                                                   
  2. One rendering engine or two?                                                                         
  3. Wayland-native main window — committed or aspirational?
  4. GTK host vs X11 native — which is the "real" backend?                                                    
  5. Is SkiaView.MauiView a public seam or internal detail?                                               
                                                                                                              
  ---                                                                                                         
  Full review (for reference / pasting into your tracker)
                                                                                                              
  # Senior-Architect Review: maui-linux                                                                   
                                                                                                              
  ## 1. What's working well (preserve these)                                                                  
                                                                                                          
  - Build/packaging clean target. OpenMaui.Controls.Linux.csproj:4 ships a single net10.0 TFM;                
  build/.props/.targets correctly handle Platforms/Linux re-inclusion when consumers use SingleProject        
  (build/OpenMaui.Controls.Linux.targets:21-49). Consumers truly do get a clean target framework — this is the
   most polished part of the project.                                                                         
  - Handler property-mapper layering follows MAUI conventions. Handlers/LayoutHandler.cs:18-26,
  Handlers/BorderHandler.cs:19-36, Handlers/ShellHandler.cs:20-36 use PropertyMapper<TVirtual,                
  THandler>(ViewHandler.ViewMapper) correctly. The mapping surface looks like every other MAUI backend — this
  is what enables future contributors to land changes without re-learning.
  - Threading model is documented and enforced via diagnostics. docs/THREADING.md is unusually good for a     
  project this size. LinuxApplication.cs:62-83 actively logs WRONG-THREAD invalidates. Most cross-platform UI 
  shims hide this — keep the heartbeat / wrong-thread warnings.                                           
  - Dispatcher fast-path is correct. Dispatching/LinuxDispatcher.cs:42-49 runs synchronously when already on
  the UI thread, and installs a SynchronizationContext (LinuxDispatcher.cs:32-34) so await continuations land 
  on the main loop. This is the right pattern.                                                            
  - EssentialsPatches is a defensible workaround. Services/EssentialsPatches.cs:29-93 uses Harmony to redirect
   MAUI's reference-assembly stubs. Ugly, but the alternative is forking MAUI Essentials. The wrapping        
  try/catch per-API means a single mis-patch doesn't kill startup.                                        
  - Dirty-region rendering with merge-threshold tuning (Rendering/SkiaRenderingEngine.cs:139-192) is the right
   shape. The merge logic is cleaner than most homegrown attempts. Region count cap (MaxDirtyRegions) prevents
   pathological invalidation storms.                                                                      
                                                 
  ## 2. Structural risks (ranked)                                                                             
                                                                                                          
  ### R1. Display-server abstraction is a Potemkin village                                                    
  - Finding: IDisplayWindow / DisplayServerFactory / WaylandDisplayWindow exist but nothing in the application
   path uses them. The app instantiates X11Window directly.                                                   
  - Evidence: LinuxApplication.cs:39 (X11Window? _mainWindow), LinuxApplication.cs:300 (new X11Window(...)).  
  SkiaRenderingEngine.cs:18,74 is parameterized on the concrete X11Window. DisplayServerFactory.CreateWindow  
  has zero call sites outside its own file. WaylandWindow.cs is 1335 lines of dead code.                      
  - Why it matters: Two costs. (a) The 1335-line WaylandWindow rots — every X11 path improvement (cursors,
  sync, IME) must be re-implemented or it'll be wrong by the time anyone tries to enable it. (b) Future       
  contributors will think the abstraction is real and waste time wiring through it.                       
  - Suggested direction: Pick a lane. Either wire IDisplayWindow through LinuxApplication and
  SkiaRenderingEngine, or move WaylandWindow.cs / WaylandDisplayWindow.cs to an experimental/ folder excluded 
  from the build, and delete DisplayServerFactory until needed.                                           
                                                 
  ### R2. Two rendering engines, only one is alive                                                            
  - Finding: GpuRenderingEngine (337 lines) and LayeredRenderer (75 lines) are never instantiated.        
  - Evidence: Grep for "new GpuRenderingEngine(" returns zero hits outside the file. LinuxApplication.cs:326  
  always constructs SkiaRenderingEngine. LinuxApplicationOptions.UseHardwareAcceleration                      
  (LinuxApplicationOptions.cs:16) is read by nothing.                                                     
  - Why it matters: GpuRenderingEngine.cs:268 admits "// Swap buffers would happen here via GLX/EGL" — there's
   no actual GPU presentation. Stub pretending to be infrastructure. Also duplicates dirty-region logic       
  (GpuRenderingEngine.cs:176-202) which will drift.                                                       
  - Suggested direction: Delete GpuRenderingEngine.cs and LayeredRenderer.cs until ready to ship GPU. When you
   are, introduce IRenderingEngine first.                                                                     
                                                                                                          
  ### R3. SkiaRenderingEngine.Current static is load-bearing
  - Finding: SkiaView, SkiaLabel, SkiaButton, SkiaEntry reach into SkiaRenderingEngine.Current?.ResourceCache 
  from Draw().                                                                                                
  - Evidence: Views/SkiaButton.cs:675,1018, Views/SkiaLabel.cs:546,684,828,975,996,                       
  Views/SkiaEntry.Drawing.cs:79,289, Views/SkiaEntry.Input.cs:414,638 — 11+ call sites.                       
  SkiaRenderingEngine.cs:39,78 defines and assigns Current as a static.                                       
  - Why it matters: Tests cannot render without constructing a real engine against an X11 window (which is why
   tests/Views/* are mostly handler-property smoke tests). Multiple windows or background rendering becomes   
  impossible without refactoring every view.                                                              
  - Suggested direction: Pass IFontResolver (or ResourceCache) explicitly into Draw(SKCanvas canvas,
  IRenderContext ctx). Mechanical refactor, low risk.                                                         
                                                                                                          
  ### R4. The Skia view tree duplicates MAUI's cross-platform layer                                           
  - Finding: SkiaView is a hand-rolled visual tree (1279 lines) with its own BindableProperty for             
  IsVisible/Opacity/WidthRequest/Margin/etc. Each handler maintains both a MauiView and a SkiaView and copies 
  properties between them.                                                                                    
  - Evidence: Views/SkiaView.cs:29-401 (~30 BindableProperty declarations duplicating View/VisualElement).
  Views/SkiaView.cs:811 (public View? MauiView). Handlers/LayoutHandler.cs:55-86,116-124 shows handlers       
  re-mapping BackgroundColor/WidthRequest/HeightRequest manually. LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:381-505   
  (RefreshViewTheme) is 120+ lines of hand-walking the SkiaView tree to forcibly re-trigger handler property
  mappers.                                                                                                    
  - Why it matters: Single biggest long-term liability. Every MAUI feature that depends on the cross-platform
  tree (AppThemeBinding, VSM, x:Bind, Triggers, Behaviors, Style inheritance, ControlTemplate, Routing) has to
   be re-implemented or proxied. Other backends (Android/iOS/Mac/Windows) have a thin platform view that holds
   NO MAUI properties; they read from the VirtualView each time.
  - Suggested direction: Team decision (Q1 below). If committing to it, formalize: SkiaView becomes a proper  
  IView with the MAUI arrange/measure contract, stop duplicating BindableProperty. If not, slim SkiaView to a 
  pure render+input node and have handlers read live from VirtualView.                                    
                                                 
  ### R5. Singleton sprawl in Services/                                                                       
  - Finding: ~16 services use the static Instance pattern AND are registered into DI as singletons.       
  - Evidence: Services/SystemThemeService.cs:21, ConnectivityService.cs:21, DeviceDisplayService.cs:16,       
  AppInfoService.cs:24, DeviceInfoService.cs:19, MonitorService.cs:28, GtkHostService.cs:17,                  
  FontFallbackManager.cs:21. Both registered in DI (Hosting/LinuxMauiAppBuilderExtensions.cs:64-102) AND  
  consumed via .Instance (LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:105,150,336, LinuxApplication.cs:343,351,             
  Handlers/GtkWebViewPlatformView.cs:111).                                                                    
  - Why it matters: Two access paths to the same object means tests can never substitute a fake.
  EssentialsPatches.cs:64-93 reaches into ConnectivityService.Instance directly — even if a consumer registers
   their own IConnectivity, Harmony routes Essentials calls to the Linux singleton.                       
  - Suggested direction: Pick one. Recommend constructor injection through MauiContext and delete .Instance
  accessors.                                                                                                  
                                                                                                          
  ### R6. LinuxTicker fires off the UI thread    
  - Finding: Animations execute on a thread pool thread, not the GTK thread.                                  
  - Evidence: Hosting/LinuxTicker.cs:32 — `_timer = new Timer(OnTimerCallback, ...)` is System.Threading.Timer
   (thread pool). OnTimerCallback invokes Fire?.Invoke() directly (Hosting/LinuxTicker.cs:43-46) without      
  dispatching.                                                                                            
  - Why it matters: Contradicts docs/THREADING.md. Any Animation.Tick consumer that touches SkiaView from this
   callback is a race.                                                                                        
  - Suggested direction: Fire?.Invoke() should go through LinuxDispatcher.Main?.Dispatch(...) or          
  GLibNative.IdleAdd. Trivial fix.                                                                            
                                                                                                              
  ### R7. Two duplicate run-loop implementations                                                              
  - Finding: LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:33-256 (Run) and Hosting/LinuxProgramHost.cs:24-88 (Run<TApp>)     
  implement parallel application bootstrap with subtly different behavior.                                    
  - Evidence: LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:104-176 wires SystemThemeService.ThemeChanged;                    
  LinuxProgramHost.RenderApplication (lines 93-156) does not.                                                 
  - Suggested direction: LinuxProgramHost.Run<TApp> should build the MauiApp and delegate to                  
  LinuxApplication.Run(app, args). One bootstrap path.                                                        
                                                                                                              
  ## 3. Specific issues that will bite soon                                                                   
                                                                                                              
  - LinuxApplication.cs:121 race on _isRedrawing (read-modify-write without lock).                            
  - LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:303-323 busy-loop: while(IsRunning) { ProcessEvents; ... Thread.Sleep(1); } 
  burns 100% of one core idle. Should poll() on the display fd.                                               
  - LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:46-47 clobbers user env: SetEnvironmentVariable("GDK_BACKEND","x11")        
  unconditional, including for users who explicitly want native Wayland GTK. At minimum a "skip if already    
  set" check.                                                                                                 
  - LinuxApplication.cs:269-273 env mutation race: setting XCURSOR_SIZE from Initialize after GTK has been
  pre-initialized at Run time may be too late — Xcursor caches at XOpenDisplay. Either set before             
  gtk_init_check or document.                                                                             
  - Hosting/LinuxViewRenderer.cs:529-548 reflection on ElementTemplate.Type: private property — will silently
  break on any MAUI minor version that renames it. Pin a test to the MAUI version.                            
  - LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:98-102, 67-69 reflection on Application.Current setter — same concern.  
  - Hosting/LinuxViewRenderer.cs:25-30, 69 static CurrentMauiShell / CurrentSkiaShell / CurrentRenderer:      
  multiple windows = silent overwrite, last-write-wins.                                                       
  - Services/Fcitx5InputMethodService.cs:66-100 dbus-monitor subprocess: forks a child process and parses 
  stdout text. Fragile to locale changes, dbus-monitor format updates, leaves orphan if app crashes. Use      
  Tmds.DBus.                                                                                                  
  - Hosting/LinuxMauiAppBuilderExtensions.cs:55 patches Essentials before DI: structurally fine, but
  EssentialsPatches.Apply() is non-idempotent across multiple MauiAppBuilder instances in the same process —  
  Harmony will refuse to re-patch.                                                                        
  - LinuxApplication.Lifecycle.cs:253 linuxApp?.Dispose() in finally runs even on clean exit, but
  LinuxApplication.cs:519-525 only nulls statics if Current == this — a second app in the same process sees   
  the field already nulled.                                                                               
                                                
  ## 4. Open architectural questions                                                                          
                                         
  Q1. Is the Skia view tree the long-term plan, or a stopgap?                                                 
  Q2. One rendering engine or two?                                                                        
  Q3. Wayland-native main window — committed or aspirational?
  Q4. GTK host vs X11 native — which is the "real" backend?                                                   
  Q5. Is MauiView a public seam (Views/SkiaView.cs:811) or implementation detail?                             
                                                                                                              
  ## 5. Recommendations, sequenced (one quarter, top 3)                                                       
                                                                                                              
  P1 — Delete dead code and pick a lane on Wayland/GPU. Effort: S (1-3 days).                                 
  Delete or move-out-of-build: GpuRenderingEngine.cs, LayeredRenderer.cs, WaylandWindow.cs,                   
  WaylandDisplayWindow.cs, DisplayServerFactory.cs, IDisplayWindow.cs, X11DisplayWindow.cs. Until ready to    
  ship, they're a tax on every change. Shrinks ~2000 lines, makes architecture legible. Caveat: if Q2/Q3      
  answer is "yes this quarter", wire them through instead.
                                                                                                              
  P2 — Fix the threading invariants the docs already promise. Effort: S (2-4 days).                           
  LinuxTicker dispatch through GTK thread. Replace while(IsRunning)+Sleep(1) with poll() on X11 display fd
  plus GLib idle queue. Fix _isRedrawing race. Validates the threading model is enforceable.                  
                                                                                                              
  P3 — Break the SkiaRenderingEngine.Current static dependency. Effort: M (1-2 weeks).                        
  Introduce per-render IRenderContext (ResourceCache, DPI scale, dirty rect) and thread it through            
  SkiaView.Draw. Mechanical but touches every view. Payoff: testable views, multi-window, GTK/X11 unification,
   GPU door open.                                                                                             
                                                 
  Stretch / next quarter: decide Q1 (Skia tree as IView vs. stopgap) and consolidate the two Run paths (R7).  
                                                                                                          
  Avoid this quarter: more controls, more services, refactoring the handler property-mapper layer.
